description: Chronological log of all hypothesis validation events, tests, and state changes

log_entries.description: Initial CAC hypothesis established from marine plasma market research
supporting_evidence: Industry benchmarks from marine plasma market sizing

log_entries.description: Initial subscription conversion rate hypothesis
CAC hypothesis revised through financial model sensitivity analysis

revision_details.what_changed: Elevated to CRITICAL severity; expanded range to $30-45
revision_details.why_changed: Sensitivity analysis shows CAC is highest-impact variable on timeline and capital
revision_details.impact: 10% CAC increase = +3 months to goal OR +$15K capital needed

log_entries.description: Subscription rate revised with recognition of uncertainty

revision_details.what_changed: Added target 60% aspirational rate vs base 50%
revision_details.why_changed: Financial model shows 50% may be conservative; 60% achievable with product excellence

log_entries.description: Monthly churn hypothesis established from financial modeling
log_entries.notes: HIGH risk: D-grade with wide range. Churn directly impacts LTV and MRR stability.

log_entries.description: Gross margin hypothesis for SaaS model
supporting_evidence: Accounting for hosting, support, payment processing

log_entries.description: Blended LTV hypothesis across subscription tiers
supporting_evidence: Calculated from churn, ARPU, and gross margin
Wide range $64-159 reflects uncertainty in inputs
log_entries.notes: CRITICAL: Composite hypothesis depending on HYP-003 (churn) and HYP-004 (margin). Chain of weak links.

log_entries.description: Organic growth lift from virality/word-of-mouth
supporting_evidence: Wide range 5-20% due to market novelty
log_entries.notes: MEDIUM severity but D-grade confidence. Conservative modeling assumes 10%.

log_entries.description: Timeline to $30K MRR hypothesis established
supporting_evidence: Range: 12 months (optimistic, 20% probability) to 24+ months (conservative, 30% probability)
log_entries.notes: CRITICAL: Invalidates original 12-month thesis. Requires business model revision.

log_entries.description: Capital sufficiency hypothesis with staged financing strategy
log_entries.notes: CRITICAL: Original $30-50K assumption insufficient. Staged financing reduces risk.

log_entries.description: Hypotheses Architecture system formally established as ontological primitive
supporting_evidence: User directive: 'This needs to be a systems architecture primitive'
Terminology shift from 'assumptions' to 'hypotheses' to reflect evolving nature
log_entries.notes: System designed for: revision history with reasoning, grades for detecting weak links, validation plans, impact severity, risk assessment, dedicated dashboard view, bidirectional indexing.

log_entries.description: CSP-001 applied to HYP-006 (Organic & Referral Lift): decomposed into 5 sub-hypotheses, surfaced 1 new hypothesis (HYP-009). Organic is not a single thing — it's 5+ distinct channels each requiring different mechanisms.
supporting_evidence: All 3 scenarios converged: organic requires 5+ distinct mechanisms (referral, SEO, influencer, PR, WOM)
log_entries.notes: Grade unchanged but now diagnostic — each sub-component independently testable. First CSP run on the system.

log_entries.description: Pre-Launch Demand Generation hypothesis surfaced by CSP-001 (SC-02 speed compression). Current system assumes zero-to-paid-acquisition on Day 1 with no pre-launch demand building.
supporting_evidence: CSP-001 SC-02 exposed missing component in growth model
Pre-launch demand building is established DTC practice
log_entries.notes: MEDIUM-HIGH severity. Reduces Day 1 CAC, provides initial organic base, creates social proof.

log_entries.description: CSP-002 applied to HYP-003 (Monthly Subscriber Churn): decomposed into 4 sub-hypotheses. Key insight: 'churn is a product hypothesis in disguise.' All 4 constraint scenarios applied — churn is the universal tax on every business model variant.
supporting_evidence: All 4 scenarios converged: product efficacy is the foundation of all retention
HYP-003.1 (Product Efficacy) carries 40% weight — most consequential finding across both CSP runs
Cross-hypothesis sub-link: HYP-006.5 depends on HYP-003.1 (no efficacy, no WOM)
New concept surfaced: Net Revenue Retention (for future scale consideration)
log_entries.notes: Composite initially 1.0 (all D). Proxy evidence then applied to upgrade HYP-003.1.

log_entries.description: HYP-003.1 (Product Efficacy & Time-to-Value) upgraded from D to C using Seaonic proxy evidence. Seaonic (near-identical product) has 4.5★ Trustpilot from 49 reviews, sustaining subscriptions at $69/box. General electrolyte VOC corpus also supports rapid result claims.

revision_details.what_changed: Grade upgraded D→C based on Seaonic proxy evidence
revision_details.why_changed: Real-world signal from near-identical product (Seaonic: 4.5★ Trustpilot, 49 reviews, sustaining subscriptions at $69/box). General electrolyte VOC shows brain fog clearing, energy, hydration claims. Not D because we have real-world signal. Not B because no IonWave-specific data.
revision_details.impact: HYP-003 composite score moves from 1.00 to 1.40. Bottleneck shifts from 'does marine plasma work?' to 'can we survive early-life churn?'

supporting_evidence: Seaonic Trustpilot: 4.5★ from 49 reviews (A-grade direct observation)
General electrolyte VOC: brain fog (VOC-002, VOC-009), energy (VOC-010), hydration (VOC-008)
log_entries.notes: Arguably the most important hypothesis in the entire system. Upgrade path: mine Seaonic review text (C→B, 2-3 hrs), then IonWave beta test (B→A).

log_entries.description: M21 (Subscription & Retention) workshop produced 5 content files providing substantive evidence for HYP-003 churn hypothesis and sub-hypotheses. Retention curve data, 9 churn prediction signals (5 missing from Danilo's model), dunning model (55-70% recovery rate), education-first win-back strategy replacing Danilo's discount ladder, and 15-tactic prioritized retention playbook. Operationalizes HYP-003.3 (Retention Infrastructure) from abstract concept to concrete implementation plan.
evidence_contributed: Industry retention curve: Month 1 (82-88%), Month 3 (60-70%), Month 6 (45-55%), Month 12 (30-40%) — validates HYP-003.2 targets
9 churn prediction signals with intervention timelines — 5 signals missing from Danilo's 4-signal model
Dunning model: 55-70% recovery rate, -1.5 to -3pp churn impact — highest single-ROI activity
Education-first win-back strategy replacing discount ladder — addresses 70-80% of churn reasons vs 15-20%
Tier 1 infrastructure expected to reduce churn from 15-20% to 10-12% (2x LTV improvement)
Scenario modeling: no infrastructure (5-6.7mo lifetime) vs Tier 1 (8.3-10mo) vs full stack (11-14mo)
log_entries.notes: Grade unchanged (still D for parent HYP-003 — no IonWave-specific data). But M21 provides the operational blueprint for testing and validating. HYP-003.3 (Retention Infrastructure) upgradeable from D to C once Tier 1 infrastructure is implemented.

log_entries.description: M21 provides subscription psychology evidence supporting HYP-002 (Subscription Attach Rate). Subscriber driver rankings (convenience > savings > consistency > identity > belonging), subscription-first funnel design recommendation (default to sub, one-time as downgrade), 6x LTV multiplier ($159 vs $26), and optimal discount range (15-20% per ProfitWell).
evidence_contributed: Subscription driver ranking: convenience > savings > consistency > identity > belonging
Subscription-first funnel recommendation: make subscription default, one-time as downgrade
6x LTV multiplier: subscribers $159 vs one-time $26
Optimal discount range: 15-20% (ProfitWell B-grade source)
Platform recommendation: Loop Subscriptions for subscription-first checkout
log_entries.notes: HYP-002 grade remains C — evidence is corroborative of existing benchmarks, not new. But M21 adds specificity: driver rankings enable funnel optimization, platform choice enables subscription-first architecture.

log_entries.description: Open issue ISS-001 flagged: 67% margin target structurally unachievable with subscription pricing. Subscription discount (15%) drops margin from 66% to 60%. At 60% subscription mix, blended margin = 62.7%. Persona dialogue (Round 4) recommended revising target to 62-64%. Kill threshold (60%) remains viable.
evidence_contributed: One-time margin: 66% ($59 price, $20 COGS)
Subscription margin: 60% ($50.15 price, $20 COGS)
Blended margin at 60% sub mix: 62.7%
HYP-004 target (67%) structurally unachievable with subscription discount
Recommendation: revise HYP-004 target to 62-64%, maintain 60% kill threshold
log_entries.notes: First open issue in the system. The 67% target was set before subscription economics were modeled. With subscription-first architecture (M10 + M21), subscription discount structurally reduces blended margin. HYP-004 needs revision, not the pricing architecture. Issue attached to HYP-004 as ISS-001.

log_entries.description: M10 provides pricing-side evidence for HYP-002 (Subscription Conversion). Subscription-first checkout design with 15% discount ($50.15/month displayed as primary), one-time at $59 as de-emphasized alternative. Price anchoring against Seaonic $69 supports subscription value perception. Planned test PT-002 (10% vs 15% subscription discount) will provide first real data.
evidence_contributed: Subscription-first checkout architecture defined
15% discount ($50.15) selected based on ProfitWell optimal range
Price testing framework established with PT-002 planned for Month 2
Cart and checkout upsell copy designed for subscription conversion
log_entries.notes: Grade remains C — architecture is designed but untested. PT-002 will provide first A/B evidence on subscription discount effectiveness.

log_entries.description: M22 (Referral & Community) workshop produced 6 content files directly operationalizing HYP-006 (Organic & Referral Lift) and sub-hypotheses HYP-006.1 (Referral) and HYP-006.5 (Community & WOM). Key contributions: referral program validated ($10/$10 store credit, phased rollout, benchmarks), community strategy with phased platform recommendation (email-first → Skool test → Circle), UGC production system (brand brief, creator program, compliance framework). Danilo overrides: removed 'more complete than LMNT' comparative claim (legal risk), added FTC compliance to all creator docs, phased community approach instead of immediate Facebook Group. 6 persona dialogue upgrades applied.
evidence_contributed: Referral program: $10/$10 store credit validated against Seed, Ritual benchmarks. Referral CAC $13.40 vs $35 paid. Phased rollout: manual codes (Month 2-3) → Smile.io (Month 3-12) → optimize
Referral benchmarks: 3-5% referral rate, 10-15% conversion, 0.15-0.30 viral coefficient (Year 1). Danilo's 0.5 viral coefficient target recalibrated as Year 2+ aspiration.
Referrer retention effect: referrers have 2-4x higher LTV, 20-30% lower churn. Referred customers +16-25% LTV, -18% churn.
Community platform evaluation: 7 platforms compared. Facebook Groups acceptable Phase 0 only. Circle recommended Phase 2 ($100K+ revenue).

UGC production system: brand brief with FTC compliance, creator brief template with content flow structure, creator program with budget tiers ($500 survival / $1K minimum viable / $2K full / $5K scale)
LMNT comparative claim REMOVED — legal risk. Replaced with unnamed comparisons.
Creator Compliance Acknowledgment requirement added for legal protection
log_entries.notes: Grade unchanged (still D for parent HYP-006 — no IonWave-specific organic/referral data). But M22 provides the operational blueprint for HYP-006.1 (referral program) and HYP-006.5 (community). These sub-hypotheses are now operationally ready for testing post-launch. Intelligence gap: Loop Subscriptions + Smile.io integration unverified.

log_entries.description: M14 Testing & Optimization workshop contributes methodology for validating HYP-001 (CAC), HYP-002 (Subscription Conversion), HYP-004 (Margin). No new evidence data — M14 provides the TESTING FRAMEWORK for generating evidence. Creative testing protocol, experimentation framework, and test queue directly target hypothesis validation.
evidence_contributed: Experimentation framework: traffic-based methodology selection (qualitative → pre/post → Bayesian → frequentist) calibrated for IonWave's low-traffic reality
Creative testing protocol: two-stage kill criteria, sequential batching for $1K budget, creative scaling protocol (graduate → ramp → stabilize → maintain)
Test queue: CT-001 (hook testing), CT-002 (UGC vs founder), LP-001 (subscription-first default), AT-001 (biohacker vs health-conscious) — all directly test hypothesis assumptions
Statistical framework: explicit error rate disclosure by confidence level, Bayesian low-conversion warning, sunk cost discipline
Testing infrastructure: phased tool stack ($0→$200/month), tool spend ≤5% of ad budget discipline
PT-000 statistical note: +23% RPV lift from $39→$49 is directional, not definitive at 75 total conversions
log_entries.quality_note: M14 is methodology, not evidence. Grade stays D because no real test data exists yet. The framework's value is in structuring HOW evidence will be generated.

log_entries.description: M3 Financial Model workshop provides CRITICAL analysis of HYP-004 (Gross Margin Sustainability). REC-001 margin dispute FULLY EXPLAINED: Danilo's 40% = fully-loaded GM (includes fulfillment, shipping, payment processing, returns). Bootstrap's 67% = product-only GM. Both correct, different definitions. Working baseline set at 60%. All margin-dependent outputs (8 of 15 tabs) show 3 scenarios. Gap traced to 5 cost categories: fulfillment ($2.50-6.00/order), shipping ($3.50-5.00), payment processing (2.9%+$0.30), returns (5-10%), subscription discount (15%). Sensitivity analysis ranks GM as #1 driver — $122K Y1 cash flow swing. Break-even analysis shows business CANNOT break even at 40% GM with 50% ad spend ratio.
evidence_contributed: REC-001 root cause analysis: 27-point margin gap is definitional (product GM vs fully-loaded GM), not a calculation error
Margin bridge: Product GM 67% → minus fulfillment 7-12% → minus shipping 6-10% → minus payment 3.4% → minus returns 5-7% → minus chargebacks 0.3-0.5% = Fully-loaded GM 34-45%
Sensitivity tornado: GM is #1 driver ($122K swing), CAC is #2 ($67K swing)
Break-even at 40% GM with 50% ad spend: IMPOSSIBLE (negative effective margin). Requires GM > 50% for paid-acquisition viability.
Two-variable matrix: GM 40% + CAC $50 = -$173K Y1 (not survivable). GM 67% + CAC $30 = -$5K Y1 (survivable).
Kill criteria mapping: RF-6 (Contribution Margin) triggered at GM < ~35%. RF-4 (Gross Margin) warning at < 60%.
Phase-gated churn threshold recommendation: M30 RF-3 (15%) too aggressive for new brand; recommend Month 1-3: 40%, Month 4-6: 30%, Month 7-12: 20%
Dialogue upgrade: Organic/renewal revenue is the most important long-term profitability driver (missing from original sensitivity analysis)
log_entries.notes: REC-001 remains BLOCKER — model uses 60% working baseline but cannot be locked until fulfillment strategy, shipping absorption policy, and return rate are confirmed with actuals. Grade elevated from D to C because the gap is now fully explained and root cause identified, even though resolution requires post-launch data. M3 is the most cross-referenced TUP in the system (draws from M2, M4, M5, M6, M10, M13, M19, M21, M24, M25, M30).
